dialogues: Look at these numbers: 9, 5, 7, 3. They're really small.
What happens if we try Scale of 10?
The bars fit, but we don't know the exact value for any of the bars.
Think about the numbers: 9, 5, 7, 3. They don't end in 0 or 5, so we know Scale of 2 is clearer to show any number. Click Scale of 2.
That's better. We can see the bars end at lines or exactly halfway. Scale of 2 could work. But when numbers are this small, you have another option. Try Scale of 1. What do you notice?
Every number lands right on a line. No halfway bars needed. We can read the graph accurately without having the data table.
For very small numbers, Scale of 1 can be a great choice. Simple and exact.
